bead.title: repo-structure: extract routing-quality ring and aggregation mechanics
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Routing-quality ring storage, aggregation, and override-class pivot mechanics still sit in the root facade rather than the ADR-008 internal package named for this responsibility.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-008-service-package-and-transcript-boundaries.md:43-64` names internal packages as the homes for concrete mechanics, including routing quality. `service.go:18-39` keeps public service methods at the root facade boundary. Routing-quality implementation remains in `service_routing_quality.go:1`, while public metric structs must stay in root.

PROPOSED FIX
Move routing-quality ring, aggregation, override-class pivot, and internal mutation/query mechanics into `internal/routingquality`. Keep public metric/request/response structs in the root package and adapt root methods to preserve external fields and JSON shape.

NON-SCOPE
Do not extract provider/model listing surfaces (`ListModels`, `ListProviders`, `HealthCheck`) except for adapter touch needed to keep compilation green. Do not refresh website docs or changelog in this slice beyond incidental references needed by tests.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / DEPENDENCY
Governing artifact: ADR-008 (`spec:ADR-008`). Parent bead/dependency: `fizeau-cd9af16b`.
bead.acceptance:
1. `service_routing_quality.go` no longer owns routing-quality ring, aggregation, or override-class pivot mechanics; those move behind `internal/routingquality` while public metric structs remain in the root package.
2. Public behavior and JSON shape for routing-quality fields exposed on public types remain unchanged.
3. Focused root test passes: `go test . -run 'TestRoutingQualityFieldsExposedOnPublicTypes' -count=1`.
4. Internal routing-quality tests pass: `go test ./internal/routingquality/... -count=1`.
5. Full repository gate passes: `go test ./... -count=1`.
6. Hook gate passes: `lefthook run pre-commit`.
